bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no time pressure to concede yet. There are no opponent offers or messages to infer their priorities, so I will start with my best values on all issues, including issueD where I am indifferent due to zero weight.

I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.02), and there is no evidence yet that the opponent is genuinely constrained beyond their first counteroffer. Their offer gives me only moderate utility mainly because it keeps issueE favorable but concedes little on my high-weight issueF and issueB, so I will hold firm on those while changing only issueD, which has zero importance to me, as a costless concession.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid while conceding only on issueD, which has zero importance to me. The opponent has held firm on most issues and only changed issueD, and their “authorized boundary” messages are cheap talk, so I will not make major concessions yet.

I am targeting essentially maximum utility because it is still very early (t=0.06) and the opponent’s offers have not improved my payoff; their latest offer actually moved issueA against my preferences. Their messages about authorization are cheap talk, so I rely on the bids: they consistently give me issueE=valueA but demand major concessions on high-weight issueF and issueB, which I should not concede this early. I will vary only issueD, which has zero weight for me, to avoid repeating my immediately previous bid while preserving my core value.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.82, keeping my most important issues issueF, issueB, and issueE at top values while making a low-cost concession on issueA and a no-cost concession on issueD. It is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s low-utility package, and their repeated “authorized limit” messages are cheap talk unsupported by meaningful concession behavior. Their offers consistently keep issueE at my preferred value but push issueF and issueC low for me, so I will hold firm there.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82, conceding only on low-importance issueA and the zero-weight issueD while holding firm on the most valuable issues F, B, E, and C. At t=0.10 there is little deadline pressure, and the opponent’s “no discretion” messages are not credible because their offers have changed while still remaining low utility for me.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.866 by conceding only on low-cost issueA and irrelevant issueD while holding my most important issues, especially issueF, issueB, and issueE. Since it is still very early at t=0.12, there is no need to make major concessions; the opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk, though their latest bid shows a small move on issueF from valueA to valueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.87 for me because it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me despite repeated “authorized limit” claims. Their bidding suggests they may care about issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueF, but I should hold firm on my highest-weight issues issueF, issueB, and issueE while conceding issueA and issueD first. All opponent offers used valid issues and values, so I continue within the valid domain.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82, conceding only on lower-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issues issueF, issueE, and issueB. At t=0.16 it is still early, so the opponent’s repeated “authorization” claims are treated as cheap talk, especially since their actual offers vary over time. Their bids consistently push issueC and issueF away from my preferences, so I am not conceding those yet, but I match their latest issueA and issueD where the cost to me is low or zero.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 because it is early in the negotiation at t=0.18, so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent’s “authorization” messages are not credible by themselves, but their bids show some movement on issueF and issueD while consistently pushing issueC low; I can concede on lower-weight issueC and the zero-weight issueD while holding firm on high-value issueB, issueE, and issueF.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.77 while making concessions mainly on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s repeated “no authority” messages are not credible because their actual offers vary; their bids suggest they may prefer issueA/valueB or valueA, issueC/valueA, issueF/valueA/B, and possibly issueB/valueB, but I am holding firm on my most important issues F, E, and B for now.

At t=0.22 it is still early enough to target a high-utility bid for me, while making concessions on lower-weight issues the opponent has repeatedly signaled interest in. I treat the opponent’s “authorization limit” messages as cheap talk, but their offers show they often prefer issueA/issueC away from my top values and do not conflict with my preferred issueE:valueA, so I concede some low-weight structure while holding firm on high-weight issueB, issueE, and issueF.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.814, conceding on lower-weight issues issueA and issueC while holding firm on the important issueB, issueE, and especially issueF. Time is still early-mid at t=0.24, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility packages; their repeated “authorization limit” messages are not credible because their actual offers have varied over time. Their latest offer moved issueC to valueB and kept issueE at my best value, so I am matching those signals where they are inexpensive for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.81 because we are only at t=0.26 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me with no reliable concession trend. I am holding firm on my highest-weight issues B, E, and F, while conceding on lower-weight A and C and giving D away since it has zero importance to me. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are not credible because their actual bids keep changing, so I am relying on the offer history rather than the claims.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.81 by conceding on lower-weight issueA and partially on issueC, while holding firm on the most important issues issueF, issueB, and issueE. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I do not need to accept the opponent’s very low offers; their repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by their changing bids, so I treat them as cheap talk.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, which is a meaningful concession from my maximum while still protecting my highest-weight issues: issueF, issueB, and issueE. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility pattern; their messages repeatedly claim no flexibility, but their actual offers vary, so I treat that as cheap talk. I concede on lower-weight issues issueA, issueC, and issueD, including values the opponent has repeatedly proposed, while holding firm on the core issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.81, keeping my high-weight issues issueF, issueB, and issueE at their best values while conceding on lower-weight issueA and partially on issueC. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I should not concede on the most important issues yet; the opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are not credible because their offers have varied. Their bids suggest they may accept issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB, while issueD is irrelevant to me and can match their latest value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.68: still well above the opponent’s recent offers to me, but with a meaningful concession on high-weight issueF from valueC to valueB. At t=0.34 there is time left, so I should not collapse to their package; their repeated “authorization” messages are contradicted by their changing bids, so I treat them as cheap talk while acknowledging their latest signal around issueF:valueB.

I am targeting a still-strong but more conciliatory bid around mid-game utility, conceding on lower-weight issueA/issueC and on issueE to valueC while holding firm on high-importance issueF and issueB. The opponent’s latest valid offer finally includes issueF: valueC, which is very valuable to me, and their recent message highlighted issueE: valueC, so this bid tests a plausible tradeoff while ignoring their repeated “authorization limit” claims as cheap talk.

I am targeting a mid-high utility around 0.65 while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid, since we are still before the late phase at t=0.38. The opponent’s offers and messages claim hard limits, but the actual bids have shifted repeatedly, including some movement toward my key preference on issueF; I therefore keep issueF and issueB strong while conceding on lower-cost issues, especially issueD which has zero weight for me.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.66, conceding on lower-cost issues and on issueB while holding firm on issueF, my most important issue. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim immovable authority, but their actual offers vary, including one at t=0.36 with issueF: valueC, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and use the bidding history instead. This bid aligns with several values the opponent has frequently proposed while preserving enough value for me at time 0.40.

I am targeting a utility around 0.736 by holding firm on my highest-weight issueF and preserving issueE, while conceding on lower or moderate issues such as issueA and issueB. At t=0.42 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s latest package; however, their latest offer included issueC:valueC and issueE:valueA, so I incorporate those signals while treating their repeated “authorization limit” messages as cheap talk contradicted by their actual movement.

I am targeting about 0.736 of my weighted utility, maintaining my strongest positions on issueF and issueE while conceding on issueB and issueA relative to my ideal. At t=0.44 we are in the mid-game, so I can make meaningful concessions but should not give up the high-weight issueF yet; the opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk, and their actual bids show some movement, especially around issueC and occasionally issueF. I also changed the zero-weight issueD to match their latest offer since it costs me nothing.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.69, keeping my highest-weight issueF at valueC while conceding on lower/medium issues the opponent has repeatedly asked for, especially issueA and issueB. At t=0.46 there is still time, so I should not drop to their preferred issueF=valueA yet; their repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk, but their bids show they can move on issueC and issueE while continuing to press on issueF.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.74 while conceding on lower-cost or opponent-signaled areas like issueB and issueD. At t=0.48 there is pressure to keep exploring compromise, but not enough to give up issueF or issueE, which are among my highest-weight issues. The opponent’s “authorization” messages are inconsistent with their changing bids, so I rely on the offer history: they have shown willingness to accept issueA/valueA, issueB/valueB, issueC/valueC, issueD/valueC, and issueE/valueA at various points, while issueF remains the main conflict.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility of about 0.69 by conceding on lower-value areas for me such as issueA and issueB while holding firm on the most important issues, especially issueF and issueE. The opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk and are contradicted by their changing bids, but their offers suggest they can work with issueB=valueB, issueE=valueA, and sometimes issueC=valueC; I also give them the latest issueD=valueB since issueD has no value to me.

I am targeting a weighted utility of about 0.77: I concede issueB to valueB, which the opponent repeatedly proposes, while holding issueF=valueC and issueE=valueA because they are my highest-weight issues and the opponent has shown some willingness to move on them. At t=0.52 there is meaningful time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility latest offer; their repeated “authorized limit” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their changing bids. This offer also incorporates their latest favorable movement on issueA=valueC and keeps issueC=valueC, which they have offered before.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.74, since we are only just past the midpoint and the opponent’s offers to me remain relatively low and inconsistent rather than steadily improving. I am conceding on lower/moderate issues such as issueA and issueB, while holding firm on my highest-value issueF and important issueE. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk, and their actual bids show some flexibility across issues, so I am not treating those claims as binding.
